title: Groundwork of Eva Foreword
wikitext:
[]: Groundwork of Eva Foreword             Quote Post by peroxid91 » Mon Oct 25, 2010 6:18 pm So I've got myself some months ago the 1st Volume of the Groundwork of Eva, and probably because I was so amazed by the sketches, I didn't noticed that the Foreword is written by Anno. But since my Japanese is nonexistent, I can't read it. Still I'm pretty curious on what he wrote down. Does anyone know if a translation of this foreword is available online? Or does someone has a knowledge in Japanese, that would be interested in translating it? Here's a picture of the foreword: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Mon Oct 25, 2010 8:16 pm Either Reichu or SSD might help you out.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Oct 25, 2010 8:50 pm I'll start. Groundwork foreword wrote: In the beginning. So to speak,

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Oct 26, 2010 11:16 am I'm far too busy to help, I just don't have the time right now. We do have a few forum members who have translated Japanese ( Reichu, EB, RockmanBalls, AnonymousEvaTranslator, Lili, IShoutedAi, Spore) or ARE Japanese like Imp, but alas, they don't post that often. Somebody will probably take interest soon enough, so just sit tight until then.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Oct 26, 2010 11:57 am My quick and superficial impression is that it's a lot of flourish, and the limited amount of potentially useful information is toward the end. I seem to vaguely recall that Eva Monkey may have had someone translate one or more of these forewords before, but he never shared with anybody.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Oct 26, 2010 12:46 pm Reichu wrote: I seem to vaguely recall that Eva Monkey may have had someone translate one or more of these forewords before, but he never shared with anybody.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Tue Oct 26, 2010 4:11 pm I can't read kana. Sorry.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Wed Oct 27, 2010 9:07 pm yare, yare~ let's see. since i have them lay in front of me, i'll do a quick translation. bear in mind there is a lot of technical mumbo-jumbo flying around so it's hard for me to translate everthing correctly but i hope you catch the drift. forword important for the so called appeal of screens are the single detailed images. particularly notable is this in the animation where the original ideas of the illustrator can be directly put on screen. the so called "image-konte" (honestly i have no idea what that means) serves as layout sheets for the detailed images and are as a template for the layout-ground sketches and the "groundwork" indispensable to animation. this very "groundwork" will be used as a basis for the finished anime later on. "time-sheet" will be added as well which is intended as a guidance for the time course. when all these processing steps are covered, the animation is taking shape for the first time. at the current workflow, the layout occurs first, then "groundwork" and lastly the "time-sheet". those responsible for all this are called animators. they have to fill the role as an illustrator, actor and cinematographer simultaneously. the true nature of animation shapes of course only with the interaction of picture, time course and audio but i would wish that one can take note of the pure, plain pencil drawings as well. and now for the "groundwork"-collection: i would have liked to bring the reader closer to the production of an anime like for example the use of colored pencils, the trivia about the creation of a layout, the time management based on the "time-sheets", as well as the basics of image and movement in animation. unfortunately because of various reasons the book only suffices for black and white print and only consists of the "groundwork". however i would still be pleased if this book could stir up some curiosity about the production of anime. all the still available "groundworks" have been collected, images from the opening up to episode 8 chosen and as much as capacity allowed printed. since the polished "groundworks" are taking the most place there are passages of less detailed images too. this results are from the partly polished works but that doesn't mean that they are incomplete. additionally i have to apologize that original images have been lost due elapsed time or that problems with the storage have occurred. also i'd like to excuse that even though several of these "groundworks" only exist as mere copies which have been polished as good as possible the visuals are not always satisfying. at this point i'd like to thank all the people who made this work possible. hideaki anno, september 4th, 2000

Article: Groundwork of Eva Foreword
 still be pleased if this book could stir up some curiosity about the production of anime. all the still available "groundworks" have been collected, images from the opening up to episode 8 chosen and as much as capacity allowed printed. since the polished "groundworks" are taking the most place there are passages of less detailed images too. this results are from the partly polished works but that doesn't mean that they are incomplete. additionally i have to apologize that original images have been lost due elapsed time or that problems with the storage have occurred. also i'd like to excuse that even though several of these "groundworks" only exist as mere copies which have been polished as good as possible the visuals are not always satisfying. at this point i'd like to thank all the people who made this work possible. hideaki anno, september 4th, 2000
